content:
Prompt 4:

Yes, the generative systems we will create will not only apply to the text, but also to the audio and visual elements. But it should come from, in some way, the input of the user or the person interacting with my work, which could also be the person who, the first living person, the first living organism, who finds the output of the machine at the end of time or something of that nature. And similarly to how you're generating your data, we could have the user, or the person who's using, or interfacing with my work, speak and interact with the characters in real time, their narrative in real time. The astrology and motion elements do play a big role in the zooming out of my thinking, which speaks to how the gods from myths were named after the planets and since the planets are always in motion and everything's always been moving in an emotion and astrology is tied directly to the planets and the gods and the future is directly tied to outer space and our exploration of space. Textually, I want the piece to be inspired by James Joyce and E. E. Cummings' minimalism in poetry and found in modern literature, the cutting and pasting like Burroughs or like how The Wasteland takes poe
